questions.q: Which empire did Babur found in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526.

questions.q: In which year did Babur win the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat in 1526 CE.

questions.q: Whom did Babur defeat at the First Battle of Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi, the last Lodi sultan of Delhi, at Panipat.

questions.q: From which broad region did Babur originally come?
questions.explanation: Babur was a Timurid prince originating from Central Asia.

questions.q: Babur wrote a famous memoir known by what name?
questions.explanation: Babur left a vivid memoir, the Baburnama, describing his life and conquests.

questions.q: Babur belonged to which dynasty by descent?
questions.explanation: Babur was a Timurid prince, descended from the line of Timur.

questions.q: Babur is remembered as both a soldier and a what?
questions.explanation: Babur was a gifted soldier and writer, famous for his memoir the Baburnama.

questions.q: Babur's victory at Panipat ended which long-standing Indian state?
questions.explanation: Babur's defeat of Ibrahim Lodi ended the Delhi Sultanate and began Mughal rule.

questions.q: Babur was the first emperor of which Indian dynasty?
questions.explanation: Babur was the founder and first emperor of the Mughal dynasty in India.

questions.q: In which century did Babur establish Mughal rule in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in 1526, in the 16th century CE.

questions.q: Which Central Asian principality did the young Babur first inherit and rule?
questions.explanation: Babur first inherited the small kingdom of Fergana as a boy.

questions.q: Which great Timurid capital did Babur repeatedly try, and ultimately fail, to hold for long?
questions.explanation: Babur captured Samarkand more than once but could never hold the storied Timurid capital.

questions.q: Which city did Babur seize and make his base before invading India?
questions.explanation: Babur captured Kabul in 1504 and used it as a base for his campaigns into India.

questions.q: On his mother's side, Babur was descended from which famous Mongol conqueror?
questions.explanation: Babur claimed descent from Genghis Khan on his mother's side and from Timur on his father's.

questions.q: Babur defeated a Rajput confederacy under Rana Sanga at which 1527 battle?
questions.explanation: Babur defeated Rana Sanga's Rajput confederacy at the Battle of Khanwa in 1527.

questions.q: In 1529 Babur defeated the Afghan forces of Bengal and Bihar at which battle?
questions.explanation: Babur defeated the eastern Afghan confederacy at the Battle of Ghaghra in 1529.

questions.q: Which military tactic and technology gave Babur a decisive edge at Panipat?
options: Field artillery and the tulughma flanking manoeuvre
questions.explanation: Babur combined field artillery with the tulughma encircling cavalry tactic to defeat a larger army.

questions.q: In which language did Babur originally write his memoirs?
questions.explanation: Babur wrote the Baburnama in Chagatai Turkic, his mother tongue.

questions.q: Who was Babur's son and immediate successor as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Babur was succeeded by his son Humayun, the second Mughal emperor.

questions.q: Babur was originally buried in Agra but, by his wish, his remains were later moved to which city?
questions.explanation: Babur was first buried at Agra and later reinterred in a garden at Kabul, as he had wished.

questions.q: Babur was fond of laying out which kind of feature wherever he settled?
questions.explanation: Babur was a keen designer of formal gardens, a passion described in the Baburnama.

questions.q: Babur captured which two principal cities of northern India after his victory at Panipat?
questions.explanation: After Panipat, Babur occupied Delhi and Agra, the heart of the former Lodi domain.

questions.q: In which year was Babur born?
questions.explanation: Babur was born in 1483 CE in Fergana, in present-day Uzbekistan.

questions.q: Babur's personal name was what, with Babur being a nickname meaning roughly tiger or beaver?
questions.explanation: Babur's formal name was Zahir-ud-din Muhammad; Babur was a nickname he carried.

questions.q: Before Khanwa, Babur is said to have publicly renounced what to rally his troops?
questions.explanation: Before Khanwa Babur dramatically renounced wine and smashed his drinking vessels to inspire his men.

questions.q: Babur's account of India in the Baburnama is noted for its detailed observation of what?
questions.explanation: The Baburnama gives detailed, often critical observations of India's plants, animals and customs.

questions.q: In which year did Babur die, after only about four years ruling in India?
questions.explanation: Babur died in 1530 CE, roughly four years after his victory at Panipat.

questions.q: According to tradition, Babur prayed to take upon himself the illness of his son, who then recovered while Babur fell ill. Who was that son?
questions.explanation: A famous tradition holds that Babur offered his own life for the recovery of his ailing son Humayun.

questions.q: Which firearms commander helped Babur deploy his artillery effectively at Panipat?
questions.explanation: The Ottoman-trained gunner Ustad Ali Quli directed Babur's artillery at Panipat.

questions.q: Which mosque, controversially attributed to Babur's reign, stood at Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The Babri Masjid at Ayodhya was traditionally attributed to Babur's reign, built by his officer Mir Baqi.
